founder.name: Sagar
founder.title: Founder & Executive Director
founder.bio: Sagar has dedicated over 15 years to advancing neuro-inclusive education across Nepal. After completing her doctorate in Special Education from Tribhuvan University and postdoctoral research in behavioral therapy in India, she returned to Nepal with a singular mission: to ensure that every child on the autism spectrum has access to dignified, evidence-based, and compassionate education. She founded NCAE in 2010 with just five students and three dedicated teachers. Today, the center serves over 120 families.

content.hero_subtitle: A structured, joyful, and evidence-based learning environment for children on the autism spectrum — right here in Nepal.
content.mission: To provide a structured, supportive environment for children on the autism spectrum in Nepal, where every learner is seen, celebrated, and given the tools to thrive on their own terms.
content.about_body: Founded in 2010, Nepal Center for Autism Excellence began with a simple but powerful belief: that every child, regardless of how their mind works, deserves a chance to learn, connect, and grow. We combine Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA), speech-language therapy, occupational therapy, and inclusive arts to build a whole-child curriculum rooted in Nepali culture and values.

programs.title: Early Intervention
programs.description: Intensive, play-based therapy sessions designed to support communication, social skills, and daily routines during the critical developmental window.

programs.title: School Readiness
programs.description: Bridging the gap between therapy and mainstream schooling through structured academic readiness, peer interaction, and self-regulation skills.

programs.title: Life Skills
programs.description: Practical independence training covering personal hygiene, community navigation, vocational exploration, and social communication for adolescents.

programs.title: Family Support
programs.description: Workshops, counseling, and a parent network to equip families with strategies, respite resources, and a community that truly understands.
